name: Philips Hue
short_description: A line of connected smart LED bulbs and accessories controlled by app, voice, and a Zigbee bridge.
long_description: Philips Hue, launched in 2012 as one of the first commercially available smart bulbs, turns ordinary room lighting into an addressable, programmable layer of the home. A central bridge links bulbs over a Zigbee mesh and exposes them to apps, schedules, sensors, and voice assistants like Alexa, Google Assistant, and Siri. As infrastructure-scale, ambient physical AI, it shows how intelligence can disappear into everyday fixtures: responding to presence, routines, and context rather than reading as a distinct device.

images.caption: A Philips Hue A19 smart LED bulb shown lit in colored light.
images.alt_text: A Philips Hue A19 screw-base smart LED bulb glowing in colored light against a neutral background.

sources.title: Introducing Philips hue: the world's smartest LED bulb, marking a new era in home lighting

interactions.title: Wake to a slow sunrise
interactions.description: Set the bedroom bulbs to fade up over 10 to 30 minutes before your alarm, mimicking a sunrise so you surface out of sleep instead of being jolted awake.

interactions.title: Wind down without blue light
interactions.description: A go-to-sleep automation dims the lights to warm tones and strips out the blue wavelengths that suppress melatonin, nudging the body toward sleep on a schedule.

interactions.title: Make an empty house look lived-in
interactions.description: While you're away, Mimic Presence cycles lights on and off across different rooms on a believable schedule so the home reads as occupied to anyone watching from outside.

interactions.title: Flash the whole house red on an intruder
interactions.description: Paired with Hue Secure cameras and contact sensors, a detected break-in can flash every bulb red and sound the siren at once, turning the lighting itself into a deterrent.

interactions.title: Light that chases the movie
interactions.description: With the Play HDMI sync box reading the video feed, color-capable bulbs throw matching light past the edges of the TV, so an explosion or a sunset spills into the room in real time.

interactions.title: Fixing a broken sleep schedule with the hidden Labs
interactions.description: A user who moved time zones rigged a Personal Wake-up Lab formula to fade the bedroom up to 70% at 8am and a time-based formula to dim to warm tones by night, and reports waking naturally on the dot for two winters running.

interactions.title: Flash the bulb when the doorbell rings
interactions.description: Home Assistant tinkerers wire a Hue bulb to a Ring doorbell's ding so the light pulses when someone is at the door — a visual alert for anyone who can't hear the chime.

interactions.title: Lights that follow you down the hall
interactions.description: Hue motion sensors already trip lights on entry; chaining a sequence of them along a hallway or stairwell could let a warm pool of light travel just ahead of you at night, then fade behind, using only motion input and the Zigbee mesh the system already runs.
